Class WorkBatch.BatchIterator

java.lang.Object
com.github.mrgarbagegamer.WorkBatch.BatchIterator
All Implemented Interfaces:
Iterator<WorkBatch.WorkItem>
Enclosing class:
WorkBatch
private class WorkBatch.BatchIterator extends Object implements Iterator<WorkBatch.WorkItem>
A reusable, allocation-free iterator that returns WorkBatch.WorkItems from the batch.

This iterator is a critical component of the zero-allocation strategy. A single instance is created per WorkBatch and reset for each iteration by WorkBatch.iterator(), avoiding the overhead of creating new iterator objects in the hot path.

Since:
2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
See Also:
  • Iterable
Performance Characteristics:
O(1) for all operations.
Threading Model:
Not thread-safe. Designed to be used by a single monkey thread at a time.
Memory Management:
Minimal and fixed memory footprint for the instance itself.

    The remove operation is not supported by this iterator, as modifying the underlying WorkBatch during iteration is not a required feature and would add complexity.

    Resets the iterator to the beginning of the batch, allowing it to be reused.
    String

    Returns a String representation of the iterator's current state, primarily for debugging.

  • Field Details

    • currentWorkItemIndex

      private int currentWorkItemIndex
      The index of the next WorkBatch.WorkItem to be returned by next(). It is incremented on each call to next and reset to 0 by reset().
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      4 bytes for the primitive int.

  • Constructor Details

    • BatchIterator

      BatchIterator()
      Constructs the iterator.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) construction.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate, apart from the instance itself.

  • Method Details

    • reset

      void reset()
      Resets the iterator to the beginning of the batch, allowing it to be reused. This is called by WorkBatch.iterator() before the iterator is returned to the caller.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) assignment.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • hasNext

      public boolean hasNext()
      Checks if there are more WorkBatch.WorkItems in the batch to iterate over.
      Specified by:
      hasNext in interface Iterator<WorkBatch.WorkItem>
      Returns:
      true if the iteration has more elements.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) comparison.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • next

      public WorkBatch.WorkItem next()
      Returns the next WorkBatch.WorkItem in the iteration. If there are no more elements, a NoSuchElementException is thrown.

      While the hasNext() call in this method seems to introduce a minor overhead, it is typically inlined and optimized away by the JVM. As such, we leave it for safety and clarity.

      Specified by:
      next in interface Iterator<WorkBatch.WorkItem>
      Returns:
      The next WorkItem.
      Throws:
      NoSuchElementException - if the iteration has no more elements.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) array access and increment.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • remove

      public void remove()
      The remove operation is not supported by this iterator, as modifying the underlying WorkBatch during iteration is not a required feature and would add complexity.
      Specified by:
      remove in interface Iterator<WorkBatch.WorkItem>
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - always.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) throw.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a String representation of the iterator's current state, primarily for debugging. The format is BatchIterator{currentWorkItemIndex=..., batch=WorkBatch@...}, with the identity hash code of the enclosing batch.
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      A String representation of the object.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1).
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Allocates a new String and StringBuilder.

Performance-optimized brute-force solver for hexagonal grid Lights Out puzzles
